error_signature: Supabase personal access tokens not working for self-hosted projects
title: Supabase plugin fails for self-hosted projects
symptom: Users cannot create new projects or edit existing ones in Weweb when using self-hosted Supabase due to the lack of personal access tokens.
not_this_if: No issues with the Weweb plugin
root_cause: Weweb 3.0 requires personal access tokens for Supabase integration, which are not available for self-hosted instances. This creates a barrier for users who need to maintain HIPAA compliance and data residency by self-hosting their Supabase projects.
fix_steps: Update to the latest version of Weweb that includes the new connection mode
Select the new connection mode that bypasses OAuth
Connect your self-hosted Supabase project without needing personal access tokens
agent_instruction: Update Weweb to the latest version and use the new connection mode for self-hosted Supabase.
